name: backup_repo_permissions.sh
msg1: ci: shellcheck integration tests (#929)\N* *: add shebang (so shellcheck can provide hints)\N* *: reset all shellcheck disables\N* *: fix issues or add disables\N* ci: add integration/tests/*.sh to shellcheck\N\NThe -x makes it follow sourced scripts.\N\N* *: make newline after shebang consistent\N* repo_health: shellcheck included scripts; too\N* repo_health: fix .studiorc shellcheck issues\N* shellcheck: add "-s bash"\N\NIt seems that the shebang of .studiorc is ignored when it's sourced by\Nsome other script; and we're asking shellcheck to follow sourced scripts\N(-ax). So; instead we're explicitly declaring the shell to assume to be\Nbash.\N\NIt does make sense; too; as without it; shellcheck will flag some\Nnon-Posix-sh stuff in that file.\N\NSigned-off-by: Stephan Renatus <srenatus@chef.io>\N
msg2: add a1 legacy token integration test (#3600)\N* add a1 legacy token integration test\N\N* backup_repo_permissions.sh: missing return 1?\N\N* ci: move a1_legacy_token to nightlies\N\N\N\NSigned-off-by: Stephan Renatus <srenatus@chef.io>

name: Test
msg1: Test: Hack #include for BSD sed build\NIn the build of the BSD sed from the Apple sources; fgetln() in main.c\Nwas returning an invalid pointer. This was due to #include'ing the GNU\N<stdio.h> rather than <bsd/stdio.h>; which apparently worked at some\Npoint in the past but is now incompatible.\NSo we hack around this by modifying the stdio.h #include to instead\Ninclude the BSD library version.\NWe also tried building instead with `-I/usr/include/bsd`; but that\Nresults in:\N    /usr/include/bsd/sys/cdefs.h:30:23: error: #include nested too deeply\Nand a slew of other errors; so it appears we need to use the BSD\Nheader files on a case-by-case basis.\N
msg2: Test: Rework MacOS testing\NWe now have two different high-level test sequences:\N1. MacOS; where we assume Bash 3 and BSD tools. `-3` is ignored\N   because the tests are the same with and without it.\N2. Non-MacOS (Linux; etc.) where we assume Bash ≥4 unless given\N   `-3`; in which case we build and use Bash 3 and the BSD tools.\NThough MacOS 10.14 (Mojave) Bash was the default shell for newly\Ncreated user accounts ; from 10.15 (Catalina) it is now zsh.[1] Since\Nthe interpreter for all our code is chosen via `#!/usr/bin/env bash`;\Nthis does not affect dot-home unless MacOS at some point stops\Ndistributing Bash 3; which seems unlikely.\N[1]: https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT208050\N

name: rc.lib
msg1: rc.*: Drop encryption support.\N1. I don't personally use this functionality. I cannot test it.\N   I cannot ensure it remains in a working and suitable condition.\N   To me personally; this is a big issue.\N2. While I have some help in this area; it's not enough for me\N   to feel comfortable keeping it in. I'd much rather drop a\N   semi-working or possibly broken feature than keep it around.\N3. This is far better suited as an external project (and by\N   extension an additional package) which can receive changes\N   independently of the init scripts.\N4. I will make any needed changes to the rc.boot/rc.shutdown\N   scripts to allow/enable encryption support to be decoupled and\N   external.\N
